Title: Morphinan biosynthesis in opium poppy requires a P450-oxidoreductase fusion protein

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Analgesic pain relief; pharmaceutical feedstock for semisynthetic opiates
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Morphinan branch of benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Gateway epimerization step from (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline in morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate branch-entry intermediate (R)-reticuline required for downstream morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially oxidizes (S)-reticuline to 1,2-dehydroreticuline and then reduces 1,2-dehydroreticuline to (R)-reticuline
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/gateway step for entry into the morphinan branch; required for morphinan alkaloid production
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 fused in-frame to an aldo-keto reductase family oxidoreductase
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Microsomal fraction; fusion protein also assayed in soluble extract for oxidoreductase activity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ethyl methanesulfonate mutagenesis used to identify loss-of-function alleles; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from stem and capsule EST/cDNA libraries; protein detected in stem extracts
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Stem and capsule; endogenous protein detected in stem
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Opium poppy cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR) as redox partner for the P450 module
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (quantitative mass spectrometry of gel-fractionated stem protein extracts); EST/transcript sequencing from stem and capsule tissue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for conversion of (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline and thereby for morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis; loss causes high (S)-reticuline with loss of morphinans
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STORR is a single fusion protein containing a CYP82Y2 P450 module and an aldo-keto reductase module. 2. The P450 module converts (S)-reticuline to 1,2-dehydroreticuline, and the oxidoreductase module converts 1,2-dehydroreticuline to (R)-reticuline. 3. storr mutant alleles abolish morphinan alkaloid production and cause accumulation of (S)-reticuline-derived alkaloids. 4. Proteomics confirmed that STORR exists in vivo as a single ~100.65-kD polypeptide. 5. The fusion architecture likely enables substrate channeling and was proposed as key to evolution of the morphinan branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant allele analysis (storr-1, storr-2, storr-3); F2 segregation and complementation tests; heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; in vitro enzyme assays; chiral HPLC-MS metabolite profiling; quantitative proteomics/mass spectrometry
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of STORR eliminates morphinan alkaloid production and redirects accumulation toward (S)-reticuline, laudanine, and laudanosine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for metabolic engineering and microbial production of morphinans because it supplies the previously missing enzymatic gateway from (S)- to (R)-reticuline; should be included when reconstructing morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: STORR was named for (S)-to-(R)-reticuline. The fusion protein directly converted (S)-reticuline to (R)-reticuline in microsomes with no detectable 1,2-dehydroreticuline accumulation, supporting substrate channeling. Similar arrangements were found in morphinan-producing Papaver species P. bracteatum and P. setigerum but not in non-morphinan-producing P. rhoeas.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides precursor for morphine, codeine, and thebaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Morphinan branch of benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream first step of the epimerization sequence
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the key gateway step but not separately established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed after heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae with cytochrome P450 reductase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from opium poppy stem cDNA and stem/capsule EST resources
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Cytochrome P450 reductase (CPR); fused to oxidoreductase module in STORR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: EST/transcript sequencing; proteomics indirectly through full-length STORR protein
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the first step converting (S)-reticuline to 1,2-dehydroreticuline; disruption prevents dehydroreticuline formation and morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP82Y2 is the P450 module within STORR. 2. storr-2 and storr-3 mutations in CYP82Y2 cause accumulation of (S)-reticuline without detectable 1,2-dehydroreticuline. 3. Heterologous expression with CPR converted 97% of (S)-reticuline to 1,2-dehydroreticuline. 4. The module functions sequentially upstream of the oxidoreductase module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; heterologous yeast expression; microsomal enzyme assay; HPLC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of CYP82Y2 activity blocks entry into the morphinan branch and causes (S)-reticuline accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential enzyme module to include with downstream morphinan genes in pathway reconstruction or microbial biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The P450 module did not function with the fused oxidoreductase as a redox partner; instead the oxidoreductase catalyzes the second reaction.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides branch-specific precursor for morphinan alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Morphinan branch of benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream second step of the epimerization sequence
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms (R)-reticuline, the gateway intermediate for morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Part of the key gateway step but not separately established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Aldo-keto reductase family, subfamily 4; closely related to codeinone reductase family
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Soluble extract when expressed separately; part of fusion protein in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Assayed after heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified from stem cDNA and stem/capsule EST resources
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Fused to CYP82Y2 module in STORR
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: EST/transcript sequencing; proteomics indirectly through full-length STORR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes reduction of 1,2-dehydroreticuline to (R)-reticuline; loss leads to dehydroreticuline accumulation and blocks morphinan production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The oxidoreductase module of STORR belongs to the aldo-keto reductase family. 2. storr-1 mutation in this module causes accumulation of 1,2-dehydroreticuline, indicating loss of the second step. 3. The module alone converted 1,2-dehydroreticuline to (R)-reticuline with 100% conversion in vitro. 4. It acts catalytically rather than as a P450 redox partner.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotype analysis; heterologous yeast expression; soluble enzyme assay; chiral HPLC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of oxidoreductase activity blocks (R)-reticuline formation and morphinan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary catalytic module for clean enzymatic production of (R)-reticuline in engineered platforms
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The oxidoreductase module was initially considered as a possible P450 redox partner but was shown to be the second catalytic step in epimerization.
